title: Father of the iPod and iPhone on building taste, judgment, and creativity in the AI era | Tony Fadell
summary: Listen now | The creator of iPod, iPhone, and Nest on opinion vs. data decisions, why marketing matters, the "three generations" rule behind everything he's shipped, and why AI will still need screens
score_reason: The topic offers a practitioner perspective on product design and decision-making in the AI era, relevant to a business and marketing audience, with actionable insights on balancing opinion and data.
partner_takeaway: Balancing opinion and data is crucial for product decisions, and AI products will still require thoughtful design around user interfaces like screens.
partner_summary: Tony Fadell, creator of the iPod and iPhone, shares his views on building taste, judgment, and creativity in the AI era. This matters to product leaders and marketers who must navigate data-driven decisions while maintaining strong design intuition. Readers should consider how to integrate human judgment with AI capabilities and why marketing remains essential even as technology evolves.

title: Building Search for AI Agents with Exa CEO Will Bryk
summary: Sarah Wang speaks with Exa cofounder and CEO Will Bryk about building search infrastructure for the AI era. The conversation covers Exa’s origins, why traditional search engines were not designed for AI agents, and how search changes when the user is no longer a human but an autonomous system. They discuss retrieval, agent workflows, coding agents, data access, and why search may become a foundational layer for the emerging agent economy. Along the way, Bryk shares his views on AI-native products, the future of information discovery, and why some of the most important problems in technology...
score_reason: The discussion on building search infrastructure for AI agents offers a strong practitioner perspective and insight into a key shift in technology impacting product and data strategy.
partner_takeaway: Search technology must evolve from human-centric models to support autonomous AI agents, changing how data retrieval and workflows are designed.
partner_summary: Will Bryk, CEO of Exa, discusses how traditional search engines are inadequate for AI agents and the need for new search infrastructure tailored to autonomous systems. This is critical for product and data leaders who must rethink search and retrieval to support AI-driven workflows. The audience should watch how AI agent-focused search evolves and consider implications for their own data and product strategies.
